items.title: Armenia’s defiant drag scene braves pushback
items.description: Armenia sits at the crossroads of Europe, Asia and the Middle East. While in much of the European Union, the drag scene is mainstream, in Armenia it remains underground. Performers face threats of violence, stigma, police pressure and even rejection by their own families. Despite this, drag queens have built a vibrant, bustling community that has attracted both local Armenians and international crowds. They make up a passionate and determined microculture challenging ultraconservative norms. And this avant garde scene is, in turn, being influenced by Armenia’s evolving political landscape.

items.title: Sick leave: Germany rising but not the worst in Europe
items.description: Sick leave in Germany has hit a new record, with workers taking an average of nearly 20 days off per year. As Chancellor Merz cracks down, how does the country's absenteeism compare to other countries?

items.title: Is Mexico the 2026 World Cup's best host country?
items.description: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Visitors are raving about the atmosphere and say they wish that there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to in the United States.

items.title: 'Hotter and hotter and hotter'
items.description: Temperature records were smashed in June - and scientists warn this is a sign of things to come.
items.source: Europe's new climate in seven charts

items.title: July 4th events threatened by heat wave. And, Russia strikes on Ukraine's capital
items.description: July 4th events for America's milestone birthday are being threatened by a brutal heat wave. And, Russia has struck Ukraine's capital, killing several people in what it calls retaliatory attacks.

items.title: AI drones made in Germany see duty on Ukraine's front line
items.description: The German defense company Helsing is supplying combat drones to Ukraine and will soon provide them to the German army. How effective are they on the front line? DW joined a Ukrainian combat mission to find out.

items.title: Argentina's economy contracted in April as uneven growth persists
items.description: Economic activity fell 1.5% from March; From a year ago, gross domestic product proxy grew 1.6%. Leer más

items.title: Trial for Bundibugyo Ebola treatment starts in DRC, WHO says
items.description: The trial ‌will evaluate Mapp Biopharmaceutical's experimental MBP134 antibody as a standalone treatment for Bundibugyo Ebola, as well as in combination with Gilead Sciences' antiviral drug remdesivir, the WHO said

items.title: Lautaro Martínez or Julián Álvarez? Scaloni weighs Messi's strike partner for Cape Verde clash

items.title: Argentines slam Milei over his handling of Cabinet chief crisis
items.description: Argentines overwhelmingly disapprove of how President Milei handled corruption scandal involving his former Cabinet chief, according to a new poll. Leer más

items.title: Welcoming the Taliban? — Germany's new Afghanistan policy
items.description: To facilitate the deportation of criminals to Afghanistan, the German government is allowing Taliban diplomats into the country.

items.title: Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz remains a powerful bargaining chip
items.description: Despite efforts by U.S. negotiators, Iran says it wants to charge a toll for ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. It's yet another unresolved issue of the U.S.-Iran war.

items.title: Iran's slain supreme leader lying in state in Tehran head of funeral events
items.description: Nearly four months after his death, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's burial is taking place as Iran and the US observe a fragile ceasefire.

items.title: After 250 years, the 'American dream' is tarnished but alive
items.description: Whatever its means, the "American dream" still offers hope to millions of people who believe the United States is a place where anyone can succeed, no matter the odds. Leer más

items.title: Turkish comedian Deniz Goktas detained at Istanbul airport
items.description: Turkish stand-up comedian Deniz Goktas was detained on his return to Turkey, days after a video he posted online gained traction. The clip included references to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and religion.

items.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Best Round of 16 knockout matches to watch
items.description: As the tournament enters the second knockout phase, there are several blockbuster matchups led by Spain-Portugal.

items.title: Two jailed for stabbing Iranian journalist in London in attack blamed on Tehran
items.description: Pouria Zeraati, a presenter at London-based Iran International, was stabbed in the leg in March 2024 outside his home in the Wimbledon area of London; Iran's senior diplomat in the U.K. has denied Tehran was behind the attack

items.title: Locals worried remote Gove Peninsular town won't survive housing crisis
items.description: A government-funded housing provider in the Northern Territory mining town of Nhulunbuy has been criticised for "not doing enough" to support small business owners.

items.title: Ukraine strikes oil terminals near St. Petersburg in Russia
items.description: The long-range strikes are the latest salvo in Ukraine’s expanding campaign to inflict economic damage on Russia and hinder its war machine.

items.title: Why tanks still matter in modern warfare
items.description: Drones, missiles and precision-guided weapons have transformed the modern battlefield, but military experts argue they cannot achieve the ultimate objective of war – controlling territory. Saurabh Trivedi examines why tanks and ground forces remain indispensable despite the rapid evolution of warfare, drawing on lessons from Ukraine and insights from senior Army officers

items.title: South Africa deploys troops as anti-migrant protests escalate
items.description: South Africa has deployed more than 3,000 soldiers across the country to reinforce security as anti-migrant protests intensify, with organisers vowing to stage demonstrations every week.

items.title: Cheers and applause as security guard rescued from rubble
items.description: Emergency workers managed to free Hernán Gil more than 100 hours after they had first located him under 140 tonnes of rubble.

items.description: Within a decade of the Civil War, the US economy had overtaken Great Britain and the entire British Empire by around 1895 – from that point onward, it was more a question of the US impact on world history than the reverse. Leer más

items.title: Arrogance as state policy and the end of Manuel Adorni
items.description: Manuel Adorni's resignation, in which he accused the press of "media attacks," comes across as pretty pathetic from a spokesman and Cabinet chief who built his tenure on arrogance and who now stands accused of corruption. Leer más

items.title: Belgian museum to make colonial-era Congo mining maps public
items.description: It is working to digitise the millions of coveted archive of century-old maps, surveys, and files of mining companies within five years.

items.title: Ounahi fires Morocco into World Cup quarterfinals with 3-0 win over Canada
items.description: Azzedine Ounahi scored two of three second-half goals as Morocco ended cohosts run to reach last eight.

items.title: Abducted Ekiti church worshippers freed after 67 days in captivity
items.description: The victims, who are mostly residents of Eda Oniyo-Ekiti, were abducted on Tuesday, April 28, when gunmen invaded an open-air crusade organised by the Christ Apostolic Church (CAC). items.title: Exclusive-World's biggest domain seller fears India's fake site crackdown could damage internet
Chancellor's message to Germany's World Cup exit backfires
items.description: "You've inspired us," read a post by Chancellor Merz on X right after Germany's lackluster performance and early elimination from the FIFA World Cup. It is seen as a communication blunder that has repercussions.
